sources.notes: For every possible root, count vertices whose subtree size is at least k and sum the answers.
Official editorial counts each vertex's contribution over all possible roots using subtree sizes from one DFS.

annotation.summary: Over all choices of the root of a tree, sum the number of vertices whose rooted subtree has size at least k.
annotation.constraints: n is large, so rerooting every root independently is too slow; each vertex's contribution must be counted directly.
annotation.core_idea: Root the tree once. For a vertex v, when the chosen root lies inside a child subtree of size s, v's rooted subtree has size n-s; otherwise it has size sub[v]. Count how many root choices make this value at least k. A DFS gives every child subtree size, and each directed edge contributes one side size to the relevant count.

solution_variants.name: subtree-contribution-count
solution_variants.summary: Compute subtree sizes once and count, for each vertex, how many root positions make its rooted subtree large enough.

tags.evidence: A single DFS computes subtree sizes, which determine every rerooted subtree size by edge-side complements.
The answer is obtained by counting each vertex's contribution over all root choices instead of rerooting explicitly.
The solution is a counting argument over possible roots and directed edge-side sizes.
